fresh_facts.claim: US Central Command conducted strikes hitting over 7,000 targets across Iran, destroying air defenses, factories, and production lines for ballistic missiles and drones.
US military forces used 5,000 lb penetrator bombs on underground coastal defense missile storage facilities in Iran.
Ballistic missile attacks on US forces decreased by 90% following US strikes on Iranian facilities.
US military conducted precision strikes hitting over 90 targets on Kar Island, including air defenses and naval bases, targeting Iranian-aligned militias in Iraq.
The US sent over 150 aircraft to recover a missing F-15E weapons systems officer following Iranian actions.
Iran's air defenses have been significantly degraded, described as 'flattened' by military assessments.
Iran's defense industrial base has been 'overwhelmingly destroyed' with severe damage to missile and drone production capabilities.
President Trump announced a 'double-sided ceasefire' between the US and Iran less than two hours before a planned strike deadline on April 10, 2026.
US-Iran ceasefire negotiations are scheduled to take place in Islamabad with US Vice President JD Vance leading the talks, including Jared Kushner.
President Trump stated the ceasefire is contingent on the Strait of Hormuz reopening and threatened 'massive strikes on power plants' or worse before the ceasefire deadline.
Israel scaled back strikes on Beirut after US pressure, with no evacuation warnings issued over a 24-hour period as of April 10.
Iran threatened to exit negotiations if Israeli bombing campaigns in Lebanon continued.
Iran disputes whether the US-Iran ceasefire covers Israeli strikes on Hezbollah in Lebanon, calling them violations of the agreement.
The US proposes a 15-point plan for ceasefire negotiations while Iran insists on its 10-point plan as the basis for talks.
President Trump vowed to keep US troops in the region until full Iranian compliance with ceasefire terms, threatening renewed military action if unmet.
A recent Iranian drone strike in Kuwait killed US personnel, with survivors disputing Pentagon details of the incident.
Iran's ambassador to Pakistan deleted a social media post related to ceasefire negotiations.
Iran is seeking sanctions relief and recovery of frozen assets as part of ceasefire negotiations.
fresh_facts.source: Government official statements and news reports

fresh_facts.claim: Disruptions to the Strait of Hormuz have caused economic shocks with lingering oil and gas price impacts.
A fragile US-Iran ceasefire began after six weeks of military fighting between the two nations.
fresh_facts.source: Government official statements and news reports

regime_fall.current_status: Following a devastating six-week military campaign that destroyed Iran's air defenses and defense industrial base, a fragile US-Iran ceasefire was announced on April 10, 2026, with negotiations in Islamabad led by VP Vance. However, Israeli strikes have resumed dramatically since mid-June 2026, assassinating senior IRGC leaders and targeting regime institutions, with Iran disputing ceasefire coverage of Israeli actions and threatening to exit negotiations.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Israeli strikes on June 14-15, 2026, assassinated IRGC Intelligence Organization Chief Brigadier General Mohammad Kazemi and killed senior military officers Mohammad Bagheri and Amir Ali Hajizadeh, while targeting the Shahran oil depot and Iranian regime institutions.
